result.summary: Found 4 visual issues on this mobile casino page, mostly related to clipped content and truncated text that appears broken rather than intentionally cropped.

issues.description: The top navigation tab 'Hot Games' is cut off mid-word, displaying as 'Hot Gam', with no visual affordance (fade, scroll indicator, or arrow) indicating that the row is horizontally scrollable.
issues.suggestion: Add a fade-out gradient or a right-side scroll indicator to communicate that the navigation tab bar is scrollable, and ensure the partially visible tab text is not misleading to users.

issues.description: Multiple game card tiles in both the 'BetRivers Exclusive Games' and 'Top Played New Games' sections are clipped at the right edge with no visible scroll cue, making the grid feel broken rather than intentionally scrollable.
issues.suggestion: Add a subtle horizontal scroll indicator or fade overlay on the right edge to signal horizontal scrollability. Ensure at least a partial peek of the next card is consistently visible.

issues.description: Game titles such as 'MAGNIFI PHO...' and 'MUNC MI...' are truncated mid-word on the partially visible right-edge cards. This looks unpolished and confusing.
issues.suggestion: Either show the full title on hover/scroll or clip at a word boundary with an ellipsis. Alternatively, ensure the card thumbnail is self-explanatory without relying on the title text.

issues.description: The bottom navigation bar icons appear tightly packed with relatively small tap targets on mobile, and the 'CASINO' label with its cherry icon uses a noticeably different visual weight (gold/active) compared to others, but the active state distinction could be clearer.
issues.suggestion: Increase spacing or tap target size for bottom navigation items to meet the 44x44px minimum touch target guideline, and ensure the active state styling is visually distinct enough for low-vision users.
